_ApproverBody.description:
Body of the approve / reject routes. Carries the second-user
id so the route layer can run `check_self_approval` against the
requester. Frozen + extra='forbid' so a stale frontend can't
smuggle additional fields.
_ApproverBody.title: _ApproverBody

_DryRunResponseV1.description:
Wire shape for ``POST .../{rule_id}/dry-run``. Returns the
list of recipients the rule WOULD dispatch to without invoking
any per-recipient handler. The shape is intentionally minimal
so a stale frontend cannot smuggle extra fields.
_DryRunResponseV1.title: _DryRunResponseV1

_FlagDetailHTTP.description:
Single shape for both list and detail responses.

Sprint 14 / S14.C: the kill-switches UI needs ``pending_first_approver_user_id``
on every row to disable the Approve CTA when the current user was the
first approver (self-second-approval is forbidden). Rather than add a
detail round-trip on click, the list returns the same shape — the
response is small (≤ a few hundred flags per tenant in the steady
state) and the cost is dwarfed by the click-then-detail latency that
would otherwise be required.
_FlagDetailHTTP.title: _FlagDetailHTTP

_GenerationIdRequest.description:
Body schema for endpoints that take only a generation_id.

The contract module ships ``StartReembedRequestV1`` /
``ActivateGenerationRequestV1`` / ``RollbackGenerationRequestV1``
but not a generic ``GenerationIdRequest`` because the cancel /
manual-retire / gc payload shapes were not promoted to a contract
in v1. They're trivial enough to declare inline with the same
Pydantic-v2 hygiene the other contracts use.
_GenerationIdRequest.title: _GenerationIdRequest

_LegacyBedrockConfigUpdateBody.description:
Legacy write shape for PUT /api/admin/bedrock-config.

Does NOT carry ``provider`` or ``role`` — those are hardcoded by the shim.
region is still required (bedrock always needs it). This class is private
to this module; the canonical contract is LlmProviderConfigUpdateV1.

api_key.description: Plaintext API key. Stored as Vault Transit ciphertext.
api_key.title: Api Key

_LegacyBedrockConfigUpdateBody.title: _LegacyBedrockConfigUpdateBody

ConfluencePageApprovalV1.description: Read shape returned from admin API. Wraps the row + revocation state.
ConfluencePageApprovalV1.title: ConfluencePageApprovalV1

CostCapChangeRequestV1.description:
`POST /api/admin/cost-caps/changes` body. Records a
requested change in `pending` state; a SECOND user must
approve before the change applies.
CostCapChangeRequestV1.title: CostCapChangeRequestV1

CostCapOverrideV1.description:
One row from `core.cost_cap_overrides` — a per-tenant
override of the per-org default cap, with optional expiry.
CostCapOverrideV1.title: CostCapOverrideV1

CostCapPageV1.description:
Top-level envelope returned by `GET /api/admin/cost-caps`.

Aggregates settings + overrides + pending changes + today's
spend snapshot in a single round-trip so the page renders
without N+1 fetches.
CostCapPageV1.title: CostCapPageV1

CostCapPendingChangeV1.description:
One row from `core.cost_cap_pending_changes`. State machine:

  pending → approved → applied
        └→ rejected
        └→ expired (24h auto-expiry)

`approved_by_user_id` is None until a second user calls the
approve route. `applied_at` is the floor at which the change
takes effect — for raises it's the approval timestamp; for
lowers it's `max(approved_at + 60min, next_midnight_utc)`.
CostCapPendingChangeV1.title: CostCapPendingChangeV1

CostCapSettingsV1.description:
Read-only snapshot of `core.cost_cap_settings`. There are
exactly two rows: scope='global' and scope='per_org_default'.
CostCapSettingsV1.title: CostCapSettingsV1

CreatePageApprovalRequestV1.description:
Admin API POST body.

Security note (audit P0-1, 2026-05-27): approver_email is INTENTIONALLY
NOT a field here. It's derived from the authenticated session in the
POST handler — never trusted from the request body. This prevents a
platform_owner from forging audit-trail entries naming a different person.
CreatePageApprovalRequestV1.title: CreatePageApprovalRequestV1

DefaultCorpusHealthV1.description: Aggregated health snapshot for the default-tag corpus.
DefaultCorpusHealthV1.title: DefaultCorpusHealthV1

DefaultScopeHitRateV1.description:
Per-scope retrieval-hit rate over the last 24h.

Hit-rate = (chunks_retrieved / chunks_in_corpus) — if the platform
has 100 default+security_only chunks and 30 of them appeared in
retrieval traces over the 24h window, hit_rate = 0.3.

Hit-rates close to 0 indicate dead weight: the scope's chunks
aren't being matched by ANY PR's effective_labels. Either retire
them or audit the labeling.
DefaultScopeHitRateV1.title: DefaultScopeHitRateV1

DetectorTrace.description: One detector's contribution to the union of effective_labels.
DetectorTrace.title: DetectorTrace

EmailRecipientV1.description:
Email recipient. ``address`` is RFC-5322 validated via Pydantic's
``EmailStr`` (backed by the ``email-validator`` package). A
typo'd address fails fast at write time.
EmailRecipientV1.title: EmailRecipientV1

EmbedderCoverageV1.description: Returned by GET /api/admin/embedder/coverage — gate for retrieval_mode flip.
EmbedderCoverageV1.title: EmbedderCoverageV1

HealthResult.description: Typed shape of a single dependency's health probe.
HealthResult.title: HealthResult

JiraRecipientV1.description:
JIRA issue-creation recipient. Carries the project key the
notification creates issues against, plus the issue_type
(Bug / Task / Story — restricted to the three the codemaster
JIRA bot is configured to author).
JiraRecipientV1.title: JiraRecipientV1

LlmProviderConfigUpdateV1.description:
WRITE shape accepted by ``PUT /api/admin/llm-provider-config``.

Carries the plaintext token. Backend pre-validates by issuing a
1-token provider ping with the new credentials before writing
anything to the DB. On validation failure: 400 with the upstream
error; no DB write; no audit row.

Cross-field invariants (enforced by model_validator):
- provider="bedrock"         → region required; model_id matches ``^(anthropic\.|claude-)``
- provider="anthropic_direct"→ region optional (ignored); model_id matches ``^claude-``

api_key.description: Plaintext LLM provider API key. Stored as Vault Transit ciphertext at rest; never returned in any GET response.
api_key.title: Api Key

model_id.description: Anthropic model id. Pattern varies by provider: bedrock accepts 'anthropic.*' or 'claude-*'; anthropic_direct accepts 'claude-*' only.
model_id.title: Model Id

region.description: AWS region (e.g., 'us-east-1'). Required for bedrock; optional (and ignored) for anthropic_direct.
region.title: Region

role.description: Credential role. PK alongside installation_id in core.llm_provider_settings.
role.title: Role

LlmProviderConfigUpdateV1.title: LlmProviderConfigUpdateV1

LlmProviderConfigV1.description:
READ shape returned by ``GET /api/admin/llm-provider-config``.

Token-free: only the fingerprint surfaces to the UI, never the
plaintext or ciphertext.

api_key_fingerprint.description: Last 4 chars of the plaintext token. The only surface piece of the token the UI ever sees.
api_key_fingerprint.title: Api Key Fingerprint

region.description: AWS region for bedrock; None for anthropic_direct.
region.title: Region

LlmProviderConfigV1.title: LlmProviderConfigV1

MembersPageV1.description:
Top-level envelope returned by `GET /api/admin/members`.

Aggregates the active member list + every in-flight pending
change in a single round-trip so the page renders without N+1
fetches.
MembersPageV1.title: MembersPageV1

MemberV1.description:
One row of the members list — a user with an active role grant.

Sourced from `core.role_grants` joined to `core.users`. The role
enum mirrors the migration 0037 (ADR-0023) reduced shape.
MemberV1.title: MemberV1

NotificationRuleCreateRequestV1.description:
``POST /api/admin/notification-rules`` body. State is
server-assigned to ``'active'`` on creation; created_at /
updated_at / rule_id are server-assigned.
NotificationRuleCreateRequestV1.title: NotificationRuleCreateRequestV1

NotificationRuleUpdateRequestV1.description:
``PATCH /api/admin/notification-rules/{rule_id}`` body. Every
field is optional; only provided fields are written through.
The Pydantic model itself enforces the discriminator on
``recipients`` and the cron validator on ``schedule_cron`` even
when the route applies a partial update.
NotificationRuleUpdateRequestV1.title: NotificationRuleUpdateRequestV1

NotificationRuleV1.description:
One row of ``core.notification_rules`` — the wire envelope
served by GET / POST / PATCH on ``/api/admin/notification-rules``.

The dispatch sink reads this envelope (re-hydrated from the
Postgres row) and fans out to the per-recipient handlers
enumerated in ``recipients``.
NotificationRuleV1.title: NotificationRuleV1

PagesListPageV1.description: Paginated envelope for the list endpoint.
PagesListPageV1.title: PagesListPageV1

PageWithApprovalV1.description:
One Confluence page in a space, with its current approval state.

``page_title`` + ``page_version`` come from the most-recent active
chunk in ``core.confluence_chunks`` (the production Postgres repo
will do the dedupe + freshness select).

``approval_status``:
  * ``approved`` — a row exists in confluence_page_approvals with
    revoked_at IS NULL.
  * ``revoked`` — a row exists but revoked_at IS NOT NULL.
  * ``none`` — no row in confluence_page_approvals for this page.

Default-labeled pages with ``approval_status != 'approved'`` are
excluded at retrieval time by the LEFT JOIN approval-drift
safeguard (Sub-spec B T10).
PageWithApprovalV1.title: PageWithApprovalV1

PatchPlatformCredentialsRequestV1.description:
PATCH body. Only supplied fields are updated. base_url + token are
independently rotatable.
PatchPlatformCredentialsRequestV1.title: PatchPlatformCredentialsRequestV1

PilotProgressV1.description: Pilot Stage 2 progress envelope for the admin Status page.

sprint_day.description: Day-of-sprint (1-based). 14-day sprints — clamps elsewhere are caller-side concerns, not contract concerns.
sprint_day.title: Sprint Day

total_orgs_onboarded.description: Distinct installations with `onboarded_at IS NOT NULL`.
total_orgs_onboarded.title: Total Orgs Onboarded

total_prs_reviewed_this_week.description: PR-review-runs that completed in the trailing 7 days.
total_prs_reviewed_this_week.title: Total Prs Reviewed This Week

PilotProgressV1.title: PilotProgressV1

PipelineStatusV1.description: Pipeline-level health summary for the admin Status page.

in_flight_review_count.description: Reviews currently being processed by Temporal workflows.
in_flight_review_count.title: In Flight Review Count

last_24h_avg_latency_seconds.description: Average end-to-end review latency in seconds, trailing 24h.
last_24h_avg_latency_seconds.title: Last 24H Avg Latency Seconds

last_24h_findings_count.description: Findings (across severities) emitted in the trailing 24 hours.
last_24h_findings_count.title: Last 24H Findings Count

last_24h_review_count.description: Reviews completed in the trailing 24 hours.
last_24h_review_count.title: Last 24H Review Count

sampled_at.description: Server-side timestamp at which the snapshot was assembled.
sampled_at.title: Sampled At

PipelineStatusV1.title: PipelineStatusV1

PlatformCredentialsMetaV1.description: Returned by GET endpoints. NEVER carries the secret value.
PlatformCredentialsMetaV1.title: PlatformCredentialsMetaV1

PullRequestRowV1.description: One PR row in the admin list view.
PullRequestRowV1.title: PullRequestRowV1

QuarantinedChunksPageV1.description: Paginated envelope for the list endpoint.
QuarantinedChunksPageV1.title: QuarantinedChunksPageV1

QuarantinedChunkV1.description: One quarantined chunk from a confluence space.
QuarantinedChunkV1.title: QuarantinedChunkV1

RepositoryEnableUpdateV1.description:
Write shape — accepts the new ``enabled`` flag.

Single-field body so the wire is unambiguous; future per-repo
settings (e.g., per-repo cost cap override) get their own
endpoints rather than expanding this one.
RepositoryEnableUpdateV1.title: RepositoryEnableUpdateV1

RepositoryV1.description: Read shape — what the PUT endpoint returns after applying the change.
RepositoryV1.title: RepositoryV1

RetrievalAggregateLineageConfidence.description:
Confidence the aggregated rows belong to the same execution lineage.

v1 always emits ``MIXED_RUN_POSSIBLE`` because trace v2 carries
``review_id`` but not ``run_id``; a review with N runs has all N
runs' traces unioned together with no way to separate them.

``EXACT`` reserved for when V3 trace contract carries ``run_id``
and the aggregator can filter to a single run.
RetrievalAggregateLineageConfidence.title: RetrievalAggregateLineageConfidence

RetrievalAggregatePRListV1.description:
Envelope: every review that has traces for a single PR.

Returned by ``GET /api/admin/retrieval-aggregates/pull-requests/{pr_id}``.
Operators use this to find candidate review_ids to drill into via
the per-review endpoint. Sorted by ``latest_captured_at DESC`` so
the most recent review appears first; ties broken by ``review_id``
ascending for determinism.

``trace_count`` per review is the UNCAPPED trace total (matches
``RetrievalAggregateV1.total_trace_count``).

Same MIXED_RUN_POSSIBLE caveat as the per-review aggregate applies:
if any review on this PR has multiple runs, ``superseded_run_count``
surfaces that count but lineage_confidence is implicit at the PR
list level (each ``RetrievalAggregatePRReviewSummaryV1`` carries
its own superseded_run_count).
RetrievalAggregatePRListV1.title: RetrievalAggregatePRListV1

RetrievalAggregatePRReviewSummaryV1.description:
Per-review summary returned by the by-PR list endpoint.

Lightweight — only the fields operators need to decide which review
to drill into via the per-review aggregate endpoint. Heavyweight
accumulators (effective_labels_union, top_spaces_retrieved,
pipeline_versions_seen) stay on the per-review aggregate.

Closes FOLLOW-UP-retrieval-aggregate-by-pr-list Phase 2.
RetrievalAggregatePRReviewSummaryV1.title: RetrievalAggregatePRReviewSummaryV1

RetrievalAggregateScope.description:
Scope of rows aggregated. v1 only emits REVIEW_SCOPED.

``RUN_SCOPED`` is reserved for the future trace V3 contract — once
``RetrievalTraceV3`` carries ``run_id`` per trace, the aggregator
can filter to one specific run and emit ``RUN_SCOPED``.
RetrievalAggregateScope.title: RetrievalAggregateScope

RetrievalAggregateV1.description:
Per-review retrieval-trace rollup. v1 = review-scoped historical
aggregate + query-time metadata snapshot.

``metadata_as_of`` is the database snapshot timestamp at which
metadata fields were read (Postgres ``CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`` from the
same CTE that read the trace rows). Historical fields are
immutable per trace row; the time window reflects MIN/MAX
``captured_at`` over the rows that successfully validated.
RetrievalAggregateV1.title: RetrievalAggregateV1

RetrievalTraceListPageV1.description: Paginated envelope for the list endpoint.
RetrievalTraceListPageV1.title: RetrievalTraceListPageV1

RetrievalTraceV2.description:
Full per-review retrieval trace — schema_version=2 (r3-#3).

Drop-in replacement for v1: same outer envelope, only Stage3 was
extended. Consumers read ``schema_version`` to dispatch.
RetrievalTraceV2.title: RetrievalTraceV2

RetrievedKnowledgeDecisionV1.description:
Per-chunk decision record (selected OR dropped).

The shape mirrors the inline ``selected_chunks_detail`` and
``dropped_chunks_detail`` dicts in spec §3.10. ``drop_reason`` is
set ONLY on dropped chunks; selected chunks have ``selected_because``
instead. All other fields are common.
RetrievedKnowledgeDecisionV1.title: RetrievedKnowledgeDecisionV1

ReviewDetailV1.description:
Typed envelope for the GET /api/admin/reviews/{id} response.

Sprint 14 / S14.B — replaces the untyped dict return shape that the
Sprint-12 endpoint helper produced. Locked + frozen so the frontend
table layout cannot drift silently.
ReviewDetailV1.title: ReviewDetailV1

ReviewFindingItemV1.description: One finding rendered on the review-detail page.
ReviewFindingItemV1.title: ReviewFindingItemV1

ReviewTimelineV1.description: Full chain envelope for a single delivery_id.

warnings.description: Sub-source unavailability messages (e.g., 'temporal SDK unreachable'). Empty tuple means every queried source returned cleanly.
warnings.title: Warnings

ReviewTimelineV1.title: ReviewTimelineV1

RoleChangePendingV1.description:
One row of `core.role_grant_pending` — a queued grant or
revoke awaiting a second-user approval.

State machine:

  pending → approved → applied
        └→ rejected
        └→ expired (TTL hit before second-user approval)

`approved_by_user_id` is None until a second user approves.
`applied_at` is the timestamp at which the row was committed
to (or removed from) `core.role_grants` inside the same
transaction as the pending → applied state flip.
RoleChangePendingV1.title: RoleChangePendingV1

RoleChangeRequestV1.description:
`POST /api/admin/members/{subject_kind}/{subject_id}/role-changes`
body. Records a requested grant or revoke in `pending` state; a
SECOND user must approve before the change applies.
RoleChangeRequestV1.title: RoleChangeRequestV1

RollbackGenerationRequestV1.description:
Rollback to any prior generation whose chunk_embeddings rows still exist
(NOT limited to 'ready' state per architect feedback #8).
RollbackGenerationRequestV1.title: RollbackGenerationRequestV1

SlackRecipientV1.description:
Slack channel recipient.

The ``channel`` field accepts either a ``#``-prefixed channel
name (e.g., ``#sec-alerts``) or a ``C``-prefixed channel id
(e.g., ``C12345`` — Slack's stable channel identifier; preferred
because channel names can be renamed and break the rule).
SlackRecipientV1.title: SlackRecipientV1

Stage3TrackTraceV2.description:
Per-track outcome with per-chunk decision detail.

Extends Sub-spec A's Stage3TrackTrace (v1) by carrying detail
arrays — the v1 selected_chunk_ids + dropped_chunk_ids are
preserved here for back-compat readers that don't yet know about
the detail arrays.
Stage3TrackTraceV2.title: Stage3TrackTraceV2

TaxonomyGapListV1.description: Top-N envelope — typically sorted by chunks_carrying DESC.
TaxonomyGapListV1.title: TaxonomyGapListV1

TaxonomySuggestionAcceptedV1.description: POST response envelope — id + queued-for-review timestamp.
TaxonomySuggestionAcceptedV1.title: TaxonomySuggestionAcceptedV1

TaxonomySuggestionV1.description:
Operator-submitted suggestion to curate a new label.

POST body to /api/admin/taxonomy/suggestions. The IDP team reviews
suggestions in their own workflow — codemaster stores them in
core.taxonomy_suggestions (a sibling table that pre-exists or that
a follow-up migration creates; the admin API contract is forward
-compatible with either).
TaxonomySuggestionV1.title: TaxonomySuggestionV1

ValidationReportV1.description:
Compatibility validation output (architect feedback #9; v4 +retrieval regression).

v4: retrieval_overlap is a HARD pass criterion — passed=True requires
overlap@5 >= 0.80 AND overlap@10 >= 0.70 (defaults; configurable via
the fixture file header).
ValidationReportV1.title: ValidationReportV1

WebhookRecipientV1.description:
HTTPS webhook recipient. ``url`` is HTTP/HTTPS-validated by
Pydantic. The webhook signing secret stays in Vault — the JSONB
persists only the Vault path so a database compromise does not
leak the secret material.
WebhookRecipientV1.title: WebhookRecipientV1

YourReviewsPageV1.description:
Per-engineer scoped reviews list for the /your-reviews page.

Two sections: reviews the user authored (PRs from them) and reviews
assigned to them (as a reviewer). Both may be independently empty.
YourReviewsPageV1.title: YourReviewsPageV1

post.description:
Run a 1-token provider ping without writing.

Returns 200 OK with `{ok: bool, message: str}` regardless of preflight
success — the frontend Test button shows either result inline. PUT
still re-runs preflight on Save (this route is convenience, not the
gate).

schema.title: Response Preflight Route Api Admin Llm Provider Config Preflight Post

get.description:
List active member grants + every in-flight pending
change for an installation. The query parameter
`installation_id` mirrors the cost-caps / status pattern —
the session-bound installation drives the body, but the
explicit query param keeps the URL deep-linkable for
super_admin cross-tenant ops.

get.description:
List all platform-scope notification rules.

Note: ``installation_id`` query param was removed in PR 2b
(platform-scope reframe). Rules are platform-shared; passing
this param returns 422 so legacy frontends surface the upgrade
rather than silently ignoring the param.

post.description:
Return the list of recipients the rule WOULD dispatch to.

Per AC #2 (sprint-19.md#L344): the synthetic-event button
on the edit page MUST run in dry-run mode so a test event
cannot accidentally post to a real Slack channel. This
endpoint returns the matched recipients without invoking
any per-recipient handler. NOT subject to audit emit (it
is read-shaped — no state mutation).

403.description: role insufficient or cross-tenant read denied

400.description: request body could not be parsed

get.description:
Phase 2 of FOLLOW-UP-retrieval-aggregate-by-pr-list.

Lists every review that has retrieval traces for the given PR,
sorted by ``latest_captured_at DESC``. Each entry carries
enough summary to decide which review_id to drill into via
the per-review endpoint at
``/api/admin/retrieval-aggregates/reviews/{review_id}``.

404.description: no traces for any review on this pr_id
